questions.scenario: A sales manager needs access to records owned by direct reports.
questions.question: Which Salesforce feature most directly supports this requirement?

glossaryTerms.definition: A Salesforce feature that grants record access upward to managers.

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: PSGs aggregate permissions but do not create hierarchical record access.
choices.whyIncorrect: Does not grant access up the management chain.
choices.directStatement: Permission Set Groups do not affect record hierarchy.

choices.label: B
choices.whyIncorrect: OWD sets baseline, not upward access.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Role hierarchy opens record access upward to managers.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: IP ranges control login, not record access.
choices.whyIncorrect: IP ranges do not affect record access.
choices.directStatement: Login IP Ranges are for login security.

questions.explanation: Role hierarchy is the most direct mechanism for upward record access through management structure.

citations.title: Salesforce Help: Control Access to Records
citations.relevanceNote: Explains record access and role hierarchy.
